title: micropholis gnaphaloclados
intro: Micropholis gnaphaloclados, also known as the white-barked angelin, is a plant species found in the tropical regions of Central and South America, and is a type of tree that belongs to the Sapotaceae family. The medicinal properties of Micropholis gnaphaloclados include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Micropholis gnaphaloclados is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
